problem.statement: The current multi-surface product preserves much of its live operational behavior but has inconsistent visual shells, weakened hierarchy, and incomplete parity after broad functional rewrites.
problem.currentWorkaround: Actors use the current functional screens despite degraded or inconsistent visual hierarchy; missing live values must remain omitted or explicitly unavailable rather than replaced with fixtures.

actors.role: Uses discovery, commerce, orders, account, notifications, support, and governed special-request journeys.
permittedActions: invoke actions backed by owned controllers
read truthful loading, empty, success, failure, forbidden, and retry states
forbiddenActions: see operator, partner, captain, or field controls
receive fixture location, metrics, catalog, order, payment, or marketing truth

actors.role: Operates authorized store, order, inventory, team, account, support, and financial-reference journeys.
permittedActions: invoke store-scoped actions backed by owned controllers
read truthful operational and financial-reference states
forbiddenActions: receive fake store, inventory, order, team, or financial truth

actors.role: Operates governed assignment, map, pickup, delivery, proof, account, support, and financial-reference journeys.
permittedActions: invoke assignment and delivery actions backed by DSH controllers
read truthful availability, GPS, assignment, and WLT-reference states
forbiddenActions: receive fake assignment, GPS, balance, ticker, badge, or availability truth

actors.role: Operates governed field onboarding, readiness, verification, catalog-assistance, finance-reference, and profile journeys.
permittedActions: invoke authorized field actions backed by owned controllers
read truthful online, offline, synchronization, and failure states
forbiddenActions: see partner, captain, or operator controls
receive fake workforce, store, catalog, visit, escalation, or financial truth

actors.role: Uses authorized DSH control-panel dashboards and operational workspaces.
permittedActions: read live controller-backed metrics and operational state
forbiddenActions: see invented KPI, health, service, financial, campaign, partner, or catalog values
bypass role boundaries or owning service contracts

routesOrScreens: shared DSH controllers and view models

surfaces.exclusionReason: The rescue must preserve and verify existing owned API bindings but does not authorize new public operations, routes, or backend behavior.
No schema, migration, seed, fixture, or production-data change is authorized by visual rescue.

outcome.statement: Every required surface retains truthful existing behavior while restoring coherent BThwani visual hierarchy, visible icons, reachable navigation, RTL, accessibility, and complete state presentation.
outcome.baselineState: Web icons are visible at the pinned commit, client and field shells remain designed, captain premium shell components are orphaned, partner semantic colors collapse hierarchy, and the operational dashboard is visually compressed.
outcome.targetState: All five required surfaces and shared UI ownership pass static, rendered, runtime, RTL, accessibility, and independent product acceptance without fixtures or backend ownership changes.
outcome.observationWindow: Same-commit targeted checks, rendered visual review, actor-specific runtime smoke, independent QA, and release verification.

criteria: Every registered shell icon renders visibly and has an accessible name where interactive.
Captain uses the governed premium header and icon-based bottom navigation without losing current assignment, delivery, identity, WLT-reference, or failure behavior.
Client preserves its governed header and bottom navigation and shows location, search, ticker, badges, or marketing state only when backed by live controllers.
Partner uses semantic UI-kit roles for background, surfaces, text, muted text, borders, success, warning, and danger without collapsing contrast.
Control-panel dashboard preserves live controller-backed metrics while restoring a rich navigable visual hierarchy without hardcoded KPI or health claims.
Field shell remains behaviorally and visually intact unless rendered evidence proves a bounded regression.
Loading, empty, success, failure, forbidden, retry, and applicable offline states remain reachable and visually distinct.
No screen, tab, CTA, controller, route, contract operation, or role-visible action is removed by the rescue.
No donor folder, fixture, preview, seed, hardcoded location, hardcoded metric, fake badge, or fake service-health claim enters runtime paths.
Reusable visual patterns remain owned by public UI-kit exports and supported shell owners.
failureStates: an icon renders as null, an empty placeholder, or an unlabeled interactive glyph
a required route, tab, action, controller binding, or actor state becomes unreachable
a visual restoration reintroduces fixture or hardcoded runtime truth
semantic roles collapse background, surface, text, border, or status contrast
a required surface is omitted without independent product approval
static checks are represented as visual, runtime, QA, release, production, or final closure
requiredChecks: targeted TypeScript checks for all affected surfaces and UI kit
UI provider, icon contract, UI-kit boundary, design token, accessibility, broken import, frontend binding, fullstack boundary, runtime anti-stub, and cleanup checks
same-commit rendered visual checks for every affected shell in RTL and applicable appearance modes
same-commit actor-specific runtime navigation and failure-state smoke
same-commit CI, release, and production evidence before final closure

business: Visual restoration preserves current service ownership, contracts, actor permissions, state machines, and live controller bindings.
DSH remains the owner of operational commerce and delivery truth while WLT remains the owner of financial truth.
Every visible metric, location, badge, ticker, health label, order, store, catalog item, financial value, and actor identity comes from an owning live state or is explicitly unavailable.
All reusable tokens, icons, primitives, and shared visual patterns remain governed through the public UI-kit contract.
negative: No hardcoded runtime KPI, location, badge count, catalog, order, store, balance, health, campaign, or actor data.
No raw network request in a screen when an owned client, adapter, or controller exists.
No role leakage or cross-surface action ownership.
No donor code dump, parallel design system, deep UI-kit import, or screen-local reusable framework.
No backend, database, dependency, lockfile, CI, secret, environment, or production-data change under this visual-rescue capability.
No deletion, move, merge, or retirement without current-commit linkage proof and a declared cleanup disposition.
No final closure without same-commit all-applicable evidence and independent approvals.

execution.appetite: Execute the rescue surface by surface on the pinned branch, preserving live behavior and stopping at the strongest independently proven stage.
fixedConstraints: Use rescue/lianbassam-fullstack-visual created from pinned lianbassam commit 8c61745ad27cea87ce3b213e051535cf0de464dd.
Do not write to lianbassam or master.
Preserve current API, controller, identity, role, DSH, WLT, and persistence ownership.
Use UI-kit tokens and public contracts.
Do not reintroduce fake, seed, fixture, preview, fallback, or hardcoded runtime truth.
Require independent approval and same-commit evidence before protected closure.
variableScope: icon selection and web compatibility rendering
shell placement of existing live states
bounded extraction of reusable visual patterns into the owning UI-kit or shell path
forbiddenScope: new backend or public API operations
self-approved product, QA, security, release, production, or final closure
execution.forecastRange: Implementation may begin only after independent Product Owner readiness approval; final closure requires the complete G0-G10 evidence chain.
unknowns: approved visual references for every affected state
independent QA, accessibility, security, release, and production decisions
live GitHub branch-protection and independent-review enforcement
